title: hive (plugin)

sections.markdown: The `hive` / `hive_flutter` packages provide a pure-Dart NoSQL key-value store. Zero native code, boxes as files on disk, fast get/put; great for settings, small caches, and simple lookups.

For sensitive data (auth tokens), wrap the box in HiveAesCipher and store the key in flutter_secure_storage. Registering adapters BEFORE openBox is essential — otherwise you get 'unknown typeId' errors on cold start.
Renumbering @HiveField ids or removing them — breaks reads of older data. Only ADD new fields with new ids; deprecate old ones with a comment.
Use lazy boxes for large values (`LazyBox<T>`) so they're not fully loaded into memory. On startup, only open the boxes you need for the first frame.

flashcards.back: Register adapters BEFORE openBox — otherwise cold reads fail with unknown typeId.
HiveAesCipher(keyBytes); store the key in flutter_secure_storage.
Only add new @HiveFields with new ids; never renumber or remove used ones.

options: Encrypted box + key in secure storage

quiz.explain: Otherwise Hive can't decode existing objects.
LazyBox avoids loading the whole box in memory.

challenges.text: Persist a theme setting with Hive.
Persist a custom User class with a generated adapter.
Encrypt a Session box; rotate the AES key.
Add a v1→v2 model migration for a widely-deployed field change.

interviewQuestions: When is Hive the right fit?
How do you encrypt Hive data?
How do you evolve the schema safely?
